Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the work done.
Question: Find the work done when a constant force $$F = 12$$ lb moves a chair from $$x = 0.9$$ to $$x = 1.1$$ ft.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the work done.
Question: How much work is done when a person lifts a $$50$$ lb box of comics onto a truck that is $$3$$ ft off the ground?

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the work done.
Question: What is the work done lifting a $$20$$ kg child from the floor to a height of $$2$$ m? (Note that a mass of $$1$$ kg weighs $$9.8$$ N near the surface of the Earth.)

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the work done.
Question: Find the work done when you push a box along the floor $$2$$ m, when you apply a constant force of $$F = 100 \text{N} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the work done.
Question: Compute the work done for a force $$F = 12 \text{/} x^{2}$$ N from $$x = 1$$ to $$x = 2$$ m.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the work done.
Question: What is the work done moving a particle from $$x = 0$$ to $$x = 1$$ m if the force acting on it is $$F = 3 x^{2}$$ N?

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the one-dimensional object.
Question: A wire that is $$2$$ ft long (starting at $$x = 0 )$$ and has a density function of $$\rho ( x ) = x^{2} + 2 x$$ lb/ft

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the one-dimensional object.
Question: A car antenna that is $$3$$ ft long (starting at $$x = 0 )$$ and has a density function of $$\rho ( x ) = 3 x + 2$$ lb/ft

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the one-dimensional object.
Question: A metal rod that is $$8$$ in. long (starting at $$x = 0 )$$ and has a density function of $$\rho ( x ) = e^{1 \text{/} 2 x}$$ lb/in.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the one-dimensional object.
Question: A pencil that is $$4$$ in. long (starting at $$x = 2 )$$ and has a density function of $$\rho ( x ) = 5 \text{/} x$$ oz/in.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the one-dimensional object.
Question: A ruler that is $$12$$ in. long (starting at $$x = 5 )$$ and has a density function of $$\rho ( x ) = \text{ln} ( x ) + ( 1 \text{/} 2 ) x^{2}$$ oz/in.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the two-dimensional object that is centered at the origin.
Question: An oversized hockey puck of radius $$2$$ in. with density function $$\rho ( x ) = x^{3} - 2 x + 5$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the two-dimensional object that is centered at the origin.
Question: A frisbee of radius $$6$$ in. with density function $$\rho ( x ) = e^{\text{unicode[:-]} x}$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the two-dimensional object that is centered at the origin.
Question: A plate of radius $$10$$ in. with density function $$\rho ( x ) = 1 + \text{cos} ( \pi x )$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the two-dimensional object that is centered at the origin.
Question: A jar lid of radius $$3$$ in. with density function $$\rho ( x ) = \text{ln} ( x + 1 )$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the two-dimensional object that is centered at the origin.
Question: A disk of radius $$5$$ cm with density function $$\rho ( x ) = \sqrt{3 x}$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the two-dimensional object that is centered at the origin.
Question: A $$12$$ -in. spring is stretched to $$15$$ in. by a force of $$75$$ lb. What is the spring constant?

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the two-dimensional object that is centered at the origin.
Question: A spring has a natural length of $$10$$ cm. It takes $$2$$ J to stretch the spring to $$15$$ cm. How much work would it take to stretch the spring from $$15$$ cm to $$20$$ cm?

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the two-dimensional object that is centered at the origin.
Question: A $$1$$ -m spring requires $$10$$ J to stretch the spring to $$1.1$$ m. How much work would it take to stretch the spring from $$1$$ m to $$1.2$$ m?

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the two-dimensional object that is centered at the origin.
Question: A spring requires $$5$$ J to stretch the spring from $$8$$ cm to $$12$$ cm, and an additional $$4$$ J to stretch the spring from $$12$$ cm to $$14$$ cm. What is the natural length of the spring?

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the two-dimensional object that is centered at the origin.
Question: A shock absorber is compressed 1 in. by a weight of 1 t. What is the spring constant?

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the two-dimensional object that is centered at the origin.
Question: A force of $$F = 20 x - x^{3}$$ N stretches a nonlinear spring by $$x$$ meters. What work is required to stretch the spring from $$x = 0$$ to $$x = 2$$ m?

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the two-dimensional object that is centered at the origin.
Question: Find the work done by winding up a hanging cable of length $$100$$ ft and weight-density $$5$$ lb/ft.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the two-dimensional object that is centered at the origin.
Question: For the cable in the preceding exercise, how much work is done to lift the cable $$50$$ ft?

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the two-dimensional object that is centered at the origin.
Question: For the cable in the preceding exercise, how much additional work is done by hanging a $$200$$ lb weight at the end of the cable?

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the two-dimensional object that is centered at the origin.
Question: [T] A pyramid of height $$500$$ ft has a square base $$800$$ ft by $$800$$ ft. Find the area $$A$$ at height $$h .$$ If the rock used to build the pyramid weighs approximately $$w = 100 \text{lb/ft}^{3} ,$$ how much work did it take to lift all the rock?

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the two-dimensional object that is centered at the origin.
Question: [T] For the pyramid in the preceding exercise, assume there were $$1000$$ workers each working $$10$$ hours a day, $$5$$ days a week, $$50$$ weeks a year. If the workers, on average, lifted 10 100 lb rocks $$2$$ ft/hr, how long did it take to build the pyramid?

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the two-dimensional object that is centered at the origin.
Question: [T] The force of gravity on a mass $$m$$ is $$F = \text{unicode[:-]} ( ( G M m ) \text{/} x^{2} )$$ newtons. For a rocket of mass $$m = 1000 \text{kg} ,$$ compute the work to lift the rocket from $$x = 6400$$ to $$x = 6500$$ km. State your answers with three significant figures. ( Note : $$G = 6.67 \times 10^{-11} \text{N m}^{2} \text{/} \text{kg}^{2}$$ and $$M = 6 \times 10^{24} \text{kg} \text{.} )$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the two-dimensional object that is centered at the origin.
Question: [T] For the rocket in the preceding exercise, find the work to lift the rocket from $$x = 6400$$ to $$x = \infty .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the two-dimensional object that is centered at the origin.
Question: [T] A rectangular dam is $$40$$ ft high and $$60$$ ft wide. Assume the weight density of water is 62.5 lbs/ft 3 . Compute the total force $$F$$ on the dam when the surface of the water is at the top of the dam and the surface of the water is halfway down the dam.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the two-dimensional object that is centered at the origin.
Question: [T] Find the work required to pump all the water out of a cylinder that has a circular base of radius $$5$$ ft and height $$200$$ ft. Use the fact that the density of water is $$62$$ lb/ft 3 .

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the two-dimensional object that is centered at the origin.
Question: [T] Find the work required to pump all the water out of the cylinder in the preceding exercise if the cylinder is only half full.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the two-dimensional object that is centered at the origin.
Question: [T] How much work is required to pump out a swimming pool if the area of the base is $$800$$ ft 2 , the water is $$4$$ ft deep, and the top is $$1$$ ft above the water level? Assume that the density of water is $$62$$ lb/ft 3 .

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the two-dimensional object that is centered at the origin.
Question: A cylinder of depth $$H$$ and cross-sectional area $$A$$ stands full of water at density $$\rho .$$ Compute the work to pump all the water to the top.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the two-dimensional object that is centered at the origin.
Question: For the cylinder in the preceding exercise, compute the work to pump all the water to the top if the cylinder is only half full.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the mass of the two-dimensional object that is centered at the origin.
Question: A cone-shaped tank has a cross-sectional area that increases with its depth: $$A = ( \pi r^{2} h^{2} ) \text{/} H^{3} .$$ Show that the work to empty it is half the work for a cylinder with the same height and base.
